case_name: Zeromska-Smith v United Lincolnshire Hospitals NHS Trust [2019] EWHC 980 (QB) (16 April 2019)

summary.brief: Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: The Claimant seeks damages for psychiatric injury arising out of the stillbirth of her daughter on 27 May 2013.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Breach of duty is admitted, as is some damage arising out of the breach of duty.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Celina Zubel, the Claimant's older half-sister, described Justyna as always being the leader and organiser in every situation.
evidence_index.speaker: Celina Zubel

evidence_index.text: She describes the change in the Claimant's personality: she says that the Claimant now loses her temper very quickly and has a short fuse.
evidence_index.speaker: Celina Zubel

evidence_index.text: Dr Baggaley considered that the pathological grief/depression was caused by the stillbirth of her daughter and the belief that it had been caused by clinical negligence.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr Martin Baggaley

evidence_index.text: The Claimant continued to suffer from her depressive illness for the rest of 2016 and remained under the care of Dr Dickinson.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr Loumidis reported to Dr Friedman that he suspected it was the supportive rather than the cognitive behavioural aspects of therapy which the Claimant was looking for.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr Loumidis

evidence_index.text: On 11 July 2017 the Claimant was dismissed from her employment on the grounds of incapacity, namely her unfitness to work on grounds of ill health consequent upon psychiatric illness.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: For the reasons stated in paragraph 104 above, this claim is disallowed in its entirety.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: I consider that the couple's counselling should be sufficient at £1,680 as Mark and Justyna's relationship will improve in any event as the therapy and recovery takes effect.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: Polish, highly educated, works as Data Manager

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: The Claimant experienced a stillbirth on 27 May 2013. She was admitted for induction of labor at term + 10 days, and a CTG trace revealed no fetal heartbeat. The baby was delivered stillborn by forceps after an 18-hour labor.

focused_exam.psychiatric: Patient exhibits symptoms of depression and pathological grief

requestables.name: Psychiatric Evaluation
requestables.description: Evaluation by Dr. Baggaley for psychiatric assessment

reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt consideration of therapy and medication

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with psychiatric symptoms following stillbirth

timeline_phases.name: Psychiatric Assessment
timeline_phases.description: Evaluation and management of psychiatric symptoms

decision_points.prompt: What therapeutic intervention should be considered?

options.clinical_reasoning: CBT is recommended for managing depression and grief
Delays necessary intervention for psychiatric symptoms

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant did not prioritize immediate therapy

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Delay in therapy exacerbated psychiatric symptoms
explanation.what_should_have_happened: CBT should have been started to manage symptoms

patient_outcome.description: Patient continued to suffer from depression and anxiety
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Chronic depression, anxiety, and impaired daily functioning

key_findings: Causation established - psychiatric injury linked to stillbirth

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to initiate timely psychiatric therapy
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Early therapy could have mitigated psychiatric symptoms

allegations.allegation: Failure to initiate timely psychiatric therapy
